title: The Battle of Bach Dang 938
summary: In 938, using the wooden stake strategy on the Bach Dang River, Ngo Quyen defeated the Southern Han army, opening an era of long-term independence for the Vietnamese nation.

slides.text: Hearing that the Southern Han army was coming to invade, Ngo Quyen came up with a brilliant strategy.
slides.imagePrompt: Ngo Quyền and his generals looking at a map of Bach Dang river, planning the trap, intelligent look, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: He ordered his troops to sharpen thousands of wooden stakes, tip them with iron, and hammer them into the bed of the Bach Dang River.
slides.imagePrompt: Vietnamese soldiers sharpening large wooden stakes and hammering them into the river bed, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: When the tide rose, the river surface hid the wooden stakes. Our troops lured the enemy warships deep into the battleground.
slides.imagePrompt: Vietnamese small boats leading large South Han warships into the trap, high tide hiding the stakes, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: As the tide receded, the enemy ships crashed against the sharp wooden stakes, breaking and sinking. Our troops launched a simultaneous attack, leading to a crushing defeat for the enemy.
slides.imagePrompt: South Han warships crashing against the wooden stakes as tide goes down, Ngo Quyen's army attacking from the banks, epic win, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: What weapon did Ngo Quyen use to hammer into the Bach Dang River?
The 938 victory on the Bach Dang River defeated which invading army?
